title: Hepatorenal Syndrome
chief_complaint: My cirrhosis patient has developed kidney failure. He's not making much urine. His belly is huge with fluid. He's on diuretics but they're not working anymore. His creatinine has doubled in 2 days.

physical_exam.General: Jaundiced, cachectic. Spider angiomata on chest.
physical_exam.Abdomen: Tense ascites. Hepatomegaly (firm, nodular). Splenomegaly. No abdominal tenderness.

hidden_data.cmp: Creatinine 3.8 (baseline 1.0 2 days ago - AKI Stage 3), BUN 68, Na 128 (dilutional hyponatremia), K+ 5.6, Albumin 2.2, Total Bilirubin 8.5, INR 2.1
hidden_data.urinalysis: Bland urine. No protein. No blood. No casts. FENa <1% (pre-renal physiology despite no response to fluids).
hidden_data.renal_us: Normal kidney size (12cm each). No hydronephrosis. Normal echogenicity.
hidden_data.fluid_challenge: No improvement in creatinine after 1.5L albumin + IV fluids challenge and diuretic withdrawal for 48h.

key_learning_points: HRS = functional renal failure from severe renal vasoconstriction in advanced cirrhosis with portal hypertension. Kidneys are structurally NORMAL (urine is bland, normal kidney size)

Type 1 HRS: Rapidly progressive (doubling Cr to >2.5 in <2 weeks). Often triggered by SBP, GI bleed, large volume paracentesis without albumin, or over-diuresis. Type 2: Slowly progressive, associated with refractory ascites

Diagnosis (ICA criteria): Cirrhosis + ascites + AKI (Cr >1.5) + No improvement after 48h diuretic withdrawal and albumin 1g/kg/day + No shock, nephrotoxins, or obstruction + No proteinuria (<500mg/day) or hematuria (<50 RBCs)

Treatment: Terlipressin (vasopressin analog) + Albumin. Midodrine + Octreotide + Albumin if Terlipressin unavailable. Norepinephrine if ICU. TIPS may improve renal function. Liver transplant is definitive treatment
Prognosis: Median survival Type 1 without treatment = 2 weeks. With treatment = 3-6 months. Liver transplant cures HRS
